---
title: "Conservative remapping"
---

Conservative remapping preserves the area-integral of a field when moving
between grids — essential for fluxes, mass, and any quantity that must be
globally conserved. The library implements it in-house by **subgrid point
sampling**, and also offers an external [CDO/SCRIP path](scrip-cdo.qmd) that is
preferred in practice for large grids.

Modules:

- `interp2D_conservative` (`src/interp2D_conservative.f90`) — the primary
  in-house implementation.
- `coordinates_mapping_conservative` (`src/coordinates_mapping_conservative.f90`)
  — an alternative field-application routine over precomputed weights.
- `coordinates_mapping.f90:758` — a third copy (`calc_weights_interpconserv1`)
  embedded in the main mapping module.

::: {.callout-note}
There are three overlapping conservative code paths and two separate
`pt_wts_class` / `calc_grid_total` definitions across these modules. Unifying
them is part of the [improvement proposal](../improvements.qmd).
:::

## Public API

| Routine | Module | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `map_conservative_init(map, grid1, grid2)` | interp2D_conservative | build overlap-area weights grid1→grid2 |
| `map_field_conservative_map(map, varname, var1, var2, fill, missing_value, mask_pack)` | interp2D_conservative | apply weights (area-weighted mean) |
| `map_field_conservative_grid(grid1, grid2, varname, var1, var2, …)` | interp2D_conservative | one-shot init+apply between two grids |
| `map_field_conservative_smooth(map, map_hilo, grid1, grid2, …)` | interp2D_conservative | conservative + smoothing variant |
| `interpconserv1_weights(x, y, dx, dy, xout, yout, dxout, dyout, latlon)` | interp2D_conservative | overlap area of source cells with one target cell |
| `map_field_conservative_map1(mp, varname, var1, var2, method, …)` | …_conservative | apply weights with `method` = mean/count/stdev |
| `calc_grid_total(x, y, var, xlim, ylim)` | both | area-integral of a field over a region |

## How the weights are computed (subgrid sampling)

For each target cell, the overlap area contributed by each nearby source cell
is estimated by dicing the source cell into a regular grid of sample points and
counting how many fall inside the target cell polygon
(`interpconserv1_weights`, `interp2D_conservative.f90:504`):

```fortran
! Target cell as a rectangular polygon
pol = create_polygon([xout±dxout/2], [yout±dyout/2])

! For each candidate source cell, sample nx×ny subgrid points
nx = max(1, int(dx/dxout))      ! (the copy in coordinates_mapping.f90 uses max(5, …))
ny = max(1, int(dy/dyout))
do j = 1, ny
  do i = 1, nx
     x1 = (x-dx/2) + dx*(i-0.5)/nx
     y1 = (y-dy/2) + dy*(j-0.5)/ny
     if (point_in_polygon(x1, y1, pol)) npts_in = npts_in + 1
  end do
end do

! Overlap area  ≈  (fraction of points inside) × source cell area
area(now) = dble(npts_in)/dble(nx*ny) * dx*dy
```

The candidate source cells are found with an axis-aligned bounding-box scan
(`which(...)`, a linear pass over all source points) sized by the coarser of
the two cell spacings.

### Applying the weights

```fortran
! area-weighted mean, skipping missing source values
var2(i) = sum(var1(ii)*area, mask=area>0) / sum(area, mask=area>0)
```

`map_field_conservative_map1` additionally offers `method="count"` (modal /
most-frequent value, weighted by area) and `method="stdev"` (area-weighted
standard deviation).

## Cost and limitations

| Property | Status |
|---|---|
| Accuracy | approximate — error ∝ 1/(subgrid points); a step function of the resolution ratio |
| Cost per target cell | O(n_neighbors × nx × ny × cost(point-in-polygon)) |
| Resolution-ratio scaling | `nx,ny ∝ dx/dxout`, so cost grows ~quadratically with the ratio (up to ~31×31 sub-points) |
| Lon-lat support | **not supported** in the embedded copy (`coordinates_mapping.f90:782`); assumes planar rectangular cells |
| Neighbor search | bounding-box linear scan, no spatial index |

This is why, for production conservative remapping on large grids, the library
shells out to `cdo` (which uses YAC's analytic spherical clipping). The
[improvement proposal](../improvements.qmd) replaces the subgrid sampling with
analytic polygon clipping so the in-house path matches CDO's accuracy and
speed, and drops the external dependency.
